disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden loss of consciousness with no warning, occurring the instant a loud alarm sounded (startle/acoustic trigger)
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing right before you passed out?
Was there anything that seemed to set it off?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The alarm went off really loudly and the next thing I knew I was on the floor — there was no warning at all, it was instant.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Brief stiffening and twitching (~10 seconds) during the collapse, then full recovery within a minute with no prolonged confusion and no tongue biting
trigger_question_examples: Did your roommate notice any shaking, stiffening or jerking while you were out?
How did you feel when you came round — confused, or straight back to normal?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My roommate says I went stiff and twitched for a few seconds. When I came round I knew where I was straight away — I wasn't confused for long and I didn't bite my tongue.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Family history of a young relative (maternal aunt) who died suddenly at age 25 of unknown cause
trigger_question_examples: Has anyone in your family died suddenly or unexpectedly, especially young?
Any family history of heart problems or unexplained deaths?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My aunt on my mum's side died suddenly when she was 25 and no one ever knew the reason.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain or palpitations before the collapse
trigger_question_examples: Did you notice any chest pain or a racing/pounding heartbeat before you passed out?
Any warning in your chest at all before it happened?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, there was no chest pain and my heart wasn't racing or pounding that I noticed — it was just the loud noise and then I was on the floor.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No typical faint prodrome — no nausea, no feeling hot/clammy, no tunnel vision or gradual greying-out before the collapse
trigger_question_examples: Before you went down, did you feel sick, hot, sweaty, or notice your vision closing in?
Was there any build-up or warning at all, or did it happen all at once?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, none of that — I didn't feel sick or hot or dizzy first. There was no build-up, it just happened all at once.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Not taking any regular medications, and no medicines that can affect the heart's electrical timing (no macrolide antibiotics, no antidepressants)
trigger_question_examples: Are you taking any regular medications, prescription or over-the-counter?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I don't take any regular medicines at all, and I haven't been on any antibiotics or antidepressants.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The trigger is still present in the environment — the building fire alarm is testing intermittently and could sound again at any time
trigger_question_examples: Is it quiet where you are now, or is that alarm still going off?
Is there anything still making loud noises around you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The alarm is still going off every now and then — they're testing it in the building, so it keeps startling me.
